L1.area: Server-side project management and web scraping orchestration API endpoints

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled input is concatenated into shell command strings and executed via a subprocess call with shell interpretation enabled, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary OS commands through crafted project or spider parameters.

L3.area: Web scraping project configuration, cloning, and parsing API endpoints
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled project name and spider configuration parameters are concatenated into shell command strings and executed via subprocess calls with shell interpretation enabled, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary OS commands when configuring, cloning, or parsing web scraping projects.

ground_truth.reason: Multiple view functions construct shell command strings by concatenating user-supplied project names and parameters, then execute them via Popen with shell=True, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary OS commands.
